citations.narrative:
The research facility did not submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report to the Deputy Administrator on or before the
extended deadline due date of 12/31/25.
Per this Section, research facilities that use or intend to use live animals in research, tests, experiments, or for teaching
shall submit an annual report, that covers the numbers of animals used during the previous Federal fiscal year, to the
Deputy Administrator on or before the December due date in this Subchapter of the AWA. Timely submission of the
annual report is essential to provide an accurate accounting of the total number of animals used and to ensure that
professionally acceptable standards governing the care and use of animals were followed during the previous Federal
fiscal year reporting period. The research facility needs to submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report. Correct by 4/22/26.

On Jan 23, 2026, a 4mo male pig, ID 45629, was found entrapped and deceased in his enclosure. Overnight the pig had
managed to manipulate the gate to his enclosure, causing it to unlatch and open. The gate was prevented from opening
fully by the glass door of secondary containment but wide enough that the pig was able to fit his head through the gap and
then under the gate, where he became entrapped between the gate and floor and asphyxiated. Failure to properly
maintain primary enclosures can lead to injury and death of the animals housed therein. The facility must be constructed
of such material and strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The housing facilities must be structurally sound
and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals from injury. Corrected prior to this inspection dated 4/8/26.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.36(a) 
   
Annual report. 
The research facility did not submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report to the Deputy Administrator on or before the 
extended deadline due date of 12/31/25. 
 
Per this Section, research facilities that use or intend to use live animals in research, tests, experiments, or for teaching 
shall submit an annual report, that covers the numbers of animals used during the previous Federal fiscal year, to the 
Deputy Administrator on or before the December due date in this Subchapter of the AWA. Timely submission of the 
annual report is essential to provide an accurate accounting of the total number of animals used and to ensure that 
professionally acceptable standards governing the care and use of animals were followed during the previous Federal 
fiscal year reporting period. The research facility needs to submit their USDA FY 25 Annual Report. Correct by 4/22/26. 
3.125(a)  Critical
   
Facilities, general. 
On Jan 23, 2026, a 4mo male pig, ID 45629, was found entrapped and deceased in his enclosure. Overnight the pig had 
managed to manipulate the gate to his enclosure, causing it to unlatch and open. The gate was prevented from opening 
fully by the glass door of secondary containment but wide enough that the pig was able to fit his head through the gap and 
then under the gate, where he became entrapped between the gate and floor and asphyxiated. Failure to properly 
maintain primary enclosures can lead to injury and death of the animals housed therein. The facility must be constructed 
of such material and strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The housing facilities must be structurally sound 
and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals from injury. Corrected prior to this inspection dated 4/8/26. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
